## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer and today we're going to talk about a huge potential problem with Metaverified. Let's go! There's a huge potential problem with Metaverified, but there's a solution. So let's think about this for a moment. The creator signs up for Metaverified. They get that nifty verification badge and all the benefits. Maybe a year, two years from now, they miss a payment or they just cancel. What happens then? I'm assuming that they lose the verification badge. That creates all kinds of confusion. Your followers suddenly think you're an imposter. They probably report you. And of course that'll require more support resources from Meta. Basically a box is you in if you ever sign up for this. You can't stop paying. The solution is simple. Once you get verified, you never lose verification assuming you keep following the rules. So you can't change your name or username for example without getting verified again. The alternative would be a one-time fee for verification. I think a lot of us would be cool with that too. Meta could still put together a subscription package that includes support, visibility and other benefits too. But tying subscription to verification just doesn't make sense. Hopefully this is something they can still change. What do you think? So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
